tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58352,"title":"[Mgmt Generator] Docs: Document ArmScopeInfo and scope handling for extension non-resource methods","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` (Azure Management SDK Generator) **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (#58291) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed PR #58291 unified scope information into a single `ArmScopeInfo` record type (renamed from a prior type) and added a `Scope: ArmScopeInfo` parameter to `NonResourceMethod`. Extension non-resource operations now carry full scope info (kind, ID pattern, resource type) through the generation pipeline, enabling correct scope parameter injection in generated mockable extension methods. Key files changed: - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Models/ResourceSchemas/ArmScopeInfo.cs` — renamed and updated unified scope record - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Models/ResourceSchemas/NonResourceMethod.cs` — now carries `ArmScopeInfo Scope` - `MockableArmClientProvider.cs` and `MockableResourceProvider.cs` — use scope info to emit the scope parameter ## Gaps Found - `docs/OperationContext.md` documents extension resources briefly (§ "Extension Resources") but does not mention `ArmScopeInfo` at all, nor does it explain how scope is resolved for **non-resource** methods on extension clients - Neither `docs/OperationContext.md` nor `docs/resolve-arm-resources-integration.md` documents the `NonResourceMethod` model or how its `Scope` field drives generated method signatures - There is no documentation of the `ArmScopeInfo` record itself: its three fields (`Kind`, `ScopeIdPattern`, `ScopeResourceType`), when `ScopeResourceType` is null, and how it differs from the per-resource `ResourceScope` enum 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` **Edit** — extend the existing "Extension Resources" section and add a new "Non-Resource Methods" subsection after "Singleton Resources": ```markdown ### ArmScopeInfo `ArmScopeInfo` is a unified record that describes the ARM scope of any operation: | Field | Type | Description | |---|---|---| | `Kind` | `ResourceScope` | Scope kind: `Tenant`, `Subscription`, `ResourceGroup`, `ManagementGroup`, or `Extension` | | `ScopeIdPattern` | `RequestPathPattern` | The parsed request path that identifies the scope (e.g., `/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}`) | | `ScopeResourceType` | `string?` | The ARM resource type of the scope (e.g., `"Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines"`). **Null** when the scope is tenant-level (empty path) or when the path contains variable resource type segments | `ArmScopeInfo` replaces the previous per-resource scope representations with a single, consistent type shared by both resource and non-resource operations. ### Non-Resource Methods Some Azure operations are **not** associated with a specific ARM resource type. They are grouped under the `NonResourceMethod` model: ``` public record NonResourceMethod( ArmScopeInfo Scope, InputServiceMethod InputMethod, InputClient InputClient) ``` The `Scope` field carries the scope at which the operation is valid. For example, a subscription-scoped non-resource list operation will have `Scope.Kind == ResourceScope.Subscription`. #### How Scope Drives Method Signatures for Non-Resource Extension Methods When a non-resource method's scope kind is `Extension` (i.e., it can be applied to any ARM resource), the generator: 1. Reads `Scope.ScopeIdPattern` to determine whether a `scope` parameter (`ResourceIdentifier`) is needed. 2. If the pattern is variable (not fixed to a specific resource type), the generated method in `MockableArmClientProvider` receives an explicit `ResourceIdentifier scope` parameter. 3. If `Scope.ScopeResourceType` is non-null, the generator can infer the scope type, and the parameter may be omitted or defaulted. **Example — generated method signature for an extension non-resource list:** ``` // Scope is Extension with variable ScopeIdPattern → explicit scope parameter public virtual AsyncPageable GetFoosAsync( ResourceIdentifier scope, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) ``` Compare to a resource-group-scoped operation where scope is contextual and the parameter is elided: ``` // Scope is ResourceGroup → scope derived from Id public virtual AsyncPageable GetFoosAsync( CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) ``` ``` #### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/resolve-arm-resources-integration.md` **Edit** — add an "ArmScopeInfo and NonResourceMethod" subsection to the "Data Structure Mapping" section: ```markdown ### ArmScopeInfo (unified scope type) `ArmScopeInfo` is produced by the emitter for both resource scopes and non-resource method scopes. It is serialised into `tspCodeModel.json` and deserialised by the C# generator: ``` interface ArmScopeInfo { kind: ResourceScope; // e.g. "Extension", "ResourceGroup", "Subscription" scopeIdPattern: string; // parsed request path for the scope scopeResourceType?: string; // ARM resource type, null for tenant or variable types } ``` ### NonResourceMethod (scope-aware) Non-resource methods are serialised with a `scope` field that contains the `ArmScopeInfo` for the operation: ``` interface NonResourceMethod { methodId: string; scope: ArmScopeInfo; } ``` The scope field is **never null** — every non-resource method must have a resolvable scope before it is included in the schema. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No code snippets are needed for these documentation-only changes. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The files being updated are generator-internal developer docs under `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/`, not a customer-facing SDK README. No standard README section check applies. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/` — ensures no C# compilation regressions 2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/test/Azure.Generator.Mgmt.Tests.csproj` — runs unit tests (168 tests expected) 3. No snippet or API export scripts are needed for documentation-only changes in `docs/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24650632981/agentic_workflow) · ● 402.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58352","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-20T05:55:07Z","updated_at":"2026-04-20T05:55:07Z"}

{"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4288781055,"number":58330,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Azure Management Generator] Docs: Missing Usage/Prerequisites section in http-client-csharp-mgmt README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` **Triggered by:** PR #58323 by `@JoshLove-msft` ## What Changed PR #58323 added `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts to both `http-client-csharp` and `http-client-csharp-mgmt` packages, and integrated doc regeneration into `Generate.ps1`. As part of this, the `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` was auto-regenerated using `tspd doc`. However, the `tspd` tool only regenerates emitter option documentation — it does not preserve or generate the human-authored `## Usage` section. ## Gaps Found - The `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is missing the `## Usage` section (with `### Prerequisite` and `### Customizing Generated Code` sub-sections) that exists in the parallel `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` - The Prerequisites sub-section (Node.js and .NET SDK version requirements) is absent, leaving mgmt emitter users without setup guidance - There is no link to a Customization Guide for mgmt-specific generated code (comparable to the one in the data-plane README) - Neither README documents the new `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts — developers modifying emitter options manually need to know they can run `npm run regen-docs` to regenerate the README
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` **Edit** — Insert the following `## Usage` section after the `## Install` section and before the `## Emitter usage` section: ```markdown ## Usage ### Prerequisite - Install [Node.js]((nodejs.org/redacted) 20 or above. (Verify by running `node --version`) - Install [**.NET 10.0 SDK**]((dotnet.microsoft.com/redacted) for your specific platform. (or a higher version within the 10.0.\* band) ### Customizing Generated Code For detailed instructions on how to customize the generated C# code for Azure management libraries, see the [Customization Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/emitter.md). ### Regenerating Documentation The emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run: ```bash npm run regen-docs ``` This rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter). ``` #### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` **Edit** — Add a "Regenerating Documentation" sub-section under `## Usage`, after `### Customizing Generated Code`: ```markdown ### Regenerating Documentation The emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run: ```bash npm run regen-docs ``` This rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter). ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Not applicable — these are tool/generator package READMEs, not Azure SDK client library packages. No snippet test files apply. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` should contain these sections after the fix: 1. `## Install` ✅ (already present) 2. `## Usage` ❌ **missing** — add it - `### Prerequisite` - `### Customizing Generated Code` - `### Regenerating Documentation` 3. `## Emitter usage` ✅ (already present) 4. `## Emitter options` ✅ (already present, auto-generated) Reference: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order from the repository root: 1. Verify the README changes look correct manually — no build is needed for pure documentation edits to these generator packages 2. Optionally, to confirm `regen-docs` still works after the edit (note: `tspd` only regenerates the `## Emitter options` section, so the `## Usage` section added manually will be preserved): ```powershell cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt npm install npm run regen-docs ``` Check that the `## Usage` section is still present in the regenerated file (it should be, since `tspd` only affects the emitter options sections it generates).

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This issue documents a gap in the Azure Management SDK Generator (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt`) documentation: the `ArmScopeInfo` record and `NonResourceMethod.Scope` field introduced in PR #58291 are not documented in `docs/OperationContext.md` or `docs/resolve-arm-resources-integration.md`.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` (Azure Management SDK Generator)
**Affected API:** `ArmScopeInfo` record, `NonResourceMethod.Scope` field, `MockableArmClientProvider`/`MockableResourceProvider`
**Scenarios:**
- `docs/OperationContext.md` has an "Extension Resources" section but does not document `ArmScopeInfo` or how scope is resolved for non-resource extension methods
- `docs/resolve-arm-resources-integration.md` does not cover the `NonResourceMethod` model or how `Scope` drives generated method signatures

**Root ask:** Add documentation for `ArmScopeInfo` and `NonResourceMethod` scope handling in the generator's developer docs
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only gap; no runtime bug is reported.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `ArmScopeInfo.cs` and `NonResourceMethod.cs` under `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Models/ResourceSchemas/` to understand the record fields
2. Update `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` with an `ArmScopeInfo` table and a "Non-Resource Methods" subsection
3. Update `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/resolve-arm-resources-integration.md` with emitter serialization details for `ArmScopeInfo` and `NonResourceMethod`
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — issue explicitly pertains to the Azure Management SDK Generator (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt`)
- **Service:** `CodeGen` — the issue is about code generation tooling in `/eng/packages/`, matching the "code generation issues (emitter, generator in /eng/packages/) → CodeGen" guideline
- **Confidence:** High — title contains "[Mgmt Generator]" and body references `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` unambiguously; similar bot-filed issues (e.g., #58330) use the same Mgmt + CodeGen label pair
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %CodeGen %Mgmt` (line 1221) — both predicted labels present, most-specific matching entry
- **AzureSdkOwners:** @live1206, @ArcturusZhang, @ArthurMa1978
- **ServiceOwners:** none listed
- **Routing action:** Assigned @live1206 (random pick from multiple AzureSdkOwners); "needs-team-attention" not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** `# ServiceLabel: %CodeGen` (line 1218) was also a candidate but was superseded by the more-specific `%CodeGen %Mgmt` entry encountered first in bottom-to-top scan
</details>
